content:
I'm currently reworking serdev abstraction.

Main issue is that after we call open() for serdev then it start call our callback functions. So this happens during probe and we have not yet set drvdata so we really cannot allow that as drvdata is needed. I can workaround and basically delay those. But I cannot know in those callbacks function that we have not yet done probe.

My current design goes that we set drvdata where we have atomic which we check in those callbacks. So now abstraction layer is also using drvdata. This works but feels over complicated.

Then I start thinking that maybe we use RegistrationOps::RegType and do struct where we have serdev + abstraction data. Then in callbacks we cast pointer which we get from serdev core back to serdev + abstraction data.

Other options which I have thinker but I do not like:

  • device::Device gives abstraction layer storage space basically abstaction_data (Kbox)
  • We ask storage space from c-layer (in this case C serdev struct)

So basically which is better for abstraction storage RegType or drvdata or do some have better idea?
Not very familar with serdev, but in geneal when probe is called the device hasn't been registered so the drvdata won't be available.
I think in your case, you'd probably want some custom data being attached to the device on the get-go when open()-ing it?

Not very familar with serdev, but in geneal when probe is called the device hasn't been registered so the
drvdata won't be available.

So during probe implementor of this abstraction is forced to call open(). We force that by having following probe signature: After that open is called serdev core will start calling other callback function even though we have not yet finish whole probe.

    fn probe(
        sdev: serdev::Opener<&serdev::Device<Core>>,
        info: Option<&Self::IdInfo>,
    ) -> impl PinInit<(Self, serdev::ProbeFinishToken), Error> {

So I need to have custom data which is only meaningful for abstraction to test if probe is finish or not. So basically AbstractionPrivateData. But question is where to store this so we can access this data during callbacks. Do we store this along with driver drvdata or along with serdev_device_driver.

Idea of storing data with serdev_device_driver:

pub struct SerdevAbstraction {
    serdev: bindings::serdev_device_driver,
    probe_done: Atomic<u32>,
}

unsafe impl<T: Driver + 'static> driver::RegistrationOps for Adapter<T> {
    type RegType = SerdevAbstraction;

    unsafe fn register(
        sdrv: &Opaque<Self::RegType>,
...

We would do register like this in callback we could cast *serdev_device_driver to SerdevAbstraction.

Or with Drvdata we would do two step initializing. This way we set_drvdata beginning of probe_callback but initialize driver field end of probe_callback

#[pin_data]
struct Drvdata<T: Driver + 'static> {
    probe_done: Atomic<u32>,
    #[pin]
    driver: UnsafeCell<MaybeUninit<(T, ProbeFinishToken)>>,
}

I'm currently leaning doing SerdevAbstraction and casting.
It seems that that SerdevAbstraction thing does not actually work. At least that Drvdata seems to work so I go with that now.

So idea is that in probe we do

            dev.set_drvdata(Drvdata::<T>::new_uninit())?;

            // SAFETY: Safe as we just set drvdata.
            let data = unsafe { dev.drvdata_borrow::<Drvdata<T>>() };

            let driver_init = T::probe(opener, info);
            // SAFETY: Only called once during of `Drvdata` lifetime.
            unsafe { data.init_driver(driver_init)? };

That way we multistate initialize drvdata an that atomic makes sure that we only access actual driver data after probe is called successfully.

Kari Argillander said:

Not very familar with serdev, but in geneal when probe is called the device hasn't been registered so the
drvdata won't be available.

So during probe implementor of this abstraction is forced to call open(). We force that by having following probe signature: After that open is called serdev core will start calling other callback function even though we have not yet finish whole probe.

    fn probe(
        sdev: serdev::Opener<&serdev::Device<Core>>,
        info: Option<&Self::IdInfo>,
    ) -> impl PinInit<(Self, serdev::ProbeFinishToken), Error> {

Token without a branded lifetime can't be used as a proof of doing something (because you could have one user funneling their token to another (using channels or using reentrance).

Perhaps a better approach to the abstraction is to first think about how would you design the Rust-side interface (without constraints from the C-side) and see how the interface layer could do.

I am also working on the rust serdev abstraction and the only remaining issue is this.
My tree: https://gitlab.com/linuxonsynology/linux/-/commits/rust_serdev.

I currently use this for testing purposes: (as device driver data)

// TODO
#[doc(hidden)]
#[pin_data]
pub struct Drvdata<T: Driver + 'static> {
    driver: UnsafeCell<Option<MaybeUninit<T>>>,
    #[pin]
    probe_complete: Completion,
}

, which is not great but works for testing.

I need to at least store one Completion before probe is run to ensure safety.
I must be able to access that Completion with just a pointer to serdev_device.
As an update: I did go with the route of adding a rust_private_data field to serdev_device.
